comps.address: Gateway Jax LaVilla (801 W Bay St)

transaction_velocity.notes: Metro Jacksonville multifamily sales declined from $3.3B (2021) to $584M through Oct 2025. Q3 2025 quarterly volume was $315M. Limited transaction data specific to Brooklyn/LaVilla submarket. Recovery expected in 2026 as market stabilizes.

distress_level.notes: No significant distress signals found for Brooklyn/LaVilla specifically. Metro-level CMBS multifamily delinquency at 6.85% (down 27bps from Oct 2025 high). Market transitioning from supply digestion to recovery phase. Limited distress-driven acquisition opportunity.

major_projects.name: Gateway Jax Pearl Square (Block N11 - 515 Pearl St)

supply_risk.notes: Significant supply pipeline in immediate zone. 61% decline in metro multifamily permits provides some moderation. Estimated 1,763+ units in immediate Brooklyn/LaVilla pipeline. However, strong demand drivers may absorb supply.

rent_growth.notes: Brooklyn 1BR rents ~$1,965 (premium to metro avg of $1,412-$1,495). Metro occupancy 92.8%. Flat to slightly positive rent growth (1-2%) after 10 consecutive quarters of flat/negative performance through Q3 2025. Recovery trajectory forming into 2026. Net absorption of 1,630 units through first 3 quarters of 2025.

major_moves.action: New graduate campus in LaVilla (20+ acres, $50M+ public funding)
$12.7M Autonomous Innovation Center opened in LaVilla; $246M Phase 2 Skyway conversion planned
Consolidated multiple facilities into new Jacksonville hub
Continued expansion as top healthcare employers

demand_drivers.notes: Jacksonville added 16,365 residents (Jul 2023-Jul 2024), ranked 10th nationally. 31,700 net in-migrants in 2024. Projected fastest-growing major FL metro through 2028. Education & health services led with 8,600 new jobs in June 2025. $8.8B downtown development pipeline. 150+ corporate HQs. UF campus is transformational catalyst for LaVilla.

capital_availability.notes: Agency lending caps increased 20.5% to $176B combined for 2026. GSEs financed $151.6B in multifamily in 2025 (+25% YoY). CMBS delinquency declining (7.14% Feb 2026 from 7.47% Jan). Multifamily led CRE transaction rebound with 51.1% YoY increase in Q3 2025. $936B in CRE mortgages maturing in 2026 creates refinancing activity. Jacksonville seen as offering cap-rate compression potential.

regulatory_favorability.notes: No rent control in Jacksonville or Florida (state preemption). 21 designated Opportunity Zones in Jacksonville (34 in Duval County). Millage rate reduced 0.125 mills (1.1%) to 11.1919 for FY26. Pro-development city council: $50M+ public investment in UF LaVilla campus, DIA actively supporting $6.5B downtown pipeline. Impact fees stable.

key_findings.finding: University of Florida securing 20+ acres in LaVilla for graduate campus with $50M+ city funding is a transformational demand catalyst. Classes started Fall 2025, new construction beginning 2026.
JTA NAVI autonomous transit system operational in LaVilla with $246M Phase 2 Skyway conversion to Holon people movers planned, connecting LaVilla to Brooklyn and Riverside.

$8.8B in downtown development projects reshaping the urban landscape, with $2.5B currently under construction. Gateway Jax Pearl Square alone bringing 1,000+ apartments to NorthCore adjacent to LaVilla.
Metro multifamily rents recovering after 10 quarters of flat/negative performance. Brooklyn commands premium rents (~$1,965 for 1BR) vs metro average (~$1,495). Occupancy at 92.8% and firming.
Jacksonville added 16,365 residents and 31,700 net in-migrants in 2024, projected fastest-growing major FL metro through 2028. Strong employment growth especially in healthcare (+8,600 jobs).

Agency lending caps increased 20.5% to $176B combined for 2026. Multifamily led CRE transaction rebound with 51.1% YoY increase nationally in Q3 2025. Jacksonville positioned for cap-rate compression.
City Council reduced millage rate by 0.125 mills (1.1%) for FY26. No rent control risk (Florida state preemption). 21 active Opportunity Zones support investment incentives.
One Riverside Phase 1 (271 units with Whole Foods anchor) now leasing in Brooklyn, validating the submarket. Phase 2 adding 115 units planned.

feature_gaps_identified.gap: No automated access to Duval County property appraiser deed transfer and lis pendens data for real-time transaction tracking
Cannot programmatically pull Jacksonville building permit data to track multifamily pipeline by subzone
No CoStar/RealPage API integration for submarket-level rent comps, occupancy, and absorption data
Unable to access CMBS loan-level data for Jacksonville-specific delinquency and special servicing metrics
No supply pipeline visualization showing unit delivery timeline against projected absorption
Missing rent growth forecast model that incorporates supply pipeline, demand drivers, and macro rates
No automated OZ investment tracking to monitor capital flows into designated census tracts
